title: CapSpec
description: JSON Schema for the Capability Description Specification (capability.json).

i18nText.description: Localized strings. Keys are locale tags (e.g. en, zh_CN). At least one locale required; en is recommended as the default.

runtime.description: Optional deployment/runtime expectations for implementations (env vars, secrets).

requiredEnvironment.description: Environment variables or platform config keys required to run a conforming implementation.

name.description: Capability name: dot-separated segments (lowerCamelCase per segment, e.g. customer.get, salesOrder.create, approval.action.approve).

category.description: Grouping label, often the domain segment.

lifecycle.description: Lifecycle stage of the capability contract.

signature.description: Contract shape: model and references to JSON Schemas for payloads. The spec fully illustrates request-response; other models may add schema pointers as needed.

model.description: Signature model (see Capability Signature Models in the CapSpec document).

requestSchema.description: URI or relative path to the request/input JSON Schema (request-response, ui-interaction).

responseSchema.description: URI or relative path to the response/output JSON Schema.

errorSchema.description: URI or relative path to the error JSON Schema.

querySchema.description: URI or relative path to the query payload JSON Schema (query-execution / data capabilities).

eventSchema.description: URI or relative path to the event payload JSON Schema (event-handler).

uiSchema.description: URI or relative path to the widget/view binding JSON Schema (ui-interaction).

idempotent.description: Repeated calls produce the same result.

sideEffect.description: Whether the capability may modify system state.

transactional.description: Whether execution must occur within a transaction.

examples.description: Example file paths and/or inline example objects.

oneOf.description: Path to an example file (e.g. examples/example1.json).

scenarios.description: Scenario instances: how the capability is used in practice (functional or non-functional).

id.description: Stable scenario identifier within the capability.

category.description: functional = business flow; non_functional = NFR (performance, security, audit, etc.).

relatedCapabilities.description: Other capability names involved in this scenario.

nfrTargets.description: Optional measurable targets for non-functional scenarios (e.g. max latency ms).

tests.description: Test artifacts: contract suite plus optional extra case files for deep conformance.

contractTests.description: Path to primary contract test YAML (tests: list).

casesDirectory.description: Optional directory (relative) containing additional case YAML files.

caseFiles.description: Paths to YAML files with top-level `cases:` list (same shape as contract test entries).
